name: Merrill Lynch Financial Advisor Colby Tragni
description_short: The Lipski Group is a Merrill Lynch Wealth Management team in Manhattan offering comprehensive financial advisory services to high-net-worth clients, business owners, and executives.
description_long:
The Lipski Group operates as part of Merrill Lynch Wealth Management, headquartered in midtown Manhattan at 114 West 47th Street, New York, NY 10036. The team is led by Senior Vice President Adam Lipski and includes First Vice President Gregg Fraistat and Vice President Colby Tragni, all recognized as Senior Financial Advisors. The group has built a reputation serving business owners, financial professionals, attorneys, and senior executives at both public and private companies in the New York City area since their establishment as a sophisticated wealth management practice.

The Lipski Group provides a comprehensive suite of wealth management and financial services including personal banking, lending products, investment solutions, retirement and savings accounts, insurance products, and wealth planning. They offer access to Merrill Lynch's full range of investment solutions combined with Bank of America's banking products. Their service model emphasizes tailored financial planning with specialized expertise in strategic planning, life insurance, corporate benefits, equity compensation, and business transitions. They also provide planning tools, educational resources through "The BullPen" insights platform, and access to events and podcasts.

The practice distinguishes itself through recognition from Forbes as a "Best-in-State Wealth Management Team" (2023-2026) and individual advisors have earned "Best-in-State Wealth Advisors" designations. Their approach centers on identifying and prioritizing client goals rather than focusing on transaction volume. They focus specifically on complex financial decisions faced by business owners and high-income professionals, offering specialized knowledge in equity compensation strategies and business transitions that general advisors may lack.

However, this is a traditional wealth management practice that requires substantial assets under management and serves primarily affluent clients. The website provides limited specific information about minimum account sizes, fee structures, or particular investment strategies. As a Merrill Lynch affiliate, they operate within a large institutional framework that may not offer the boutique personalization some clients seek. This firm is fundamentally mischaracterized in the "credit-repair" category and is not a credit improvement or consumer finance company.
address: 1 Bryant Pk 3rd Floor, New York, NY 10036

company_info.headquarters: 1 Bryant Pk 3rd Floor, New York, NY 10036

pros: Forbes-recognized wealth management team with consistent annual rankings (2023-2026) for best-in-state teams and advisors
Specialized expertise in complex areas like equity compensation, business transitions, and corporate benefits planning
Access to comprehensive Merrill Lynch investment solutions combined with Bank of America banking and lending products
Senior advisor team with established track records—Adam Lipski recognized as "Top Next-Gen Wealth Advisor" in 2024
Personalized goal-based approach that prioritizes client objectives over transaction-focused relationships
Located in Manhattan with local expertise serving NYC-area business owners, executives, and high-income professionals
Integrated wealth planning across investments, retirement, insurance, lending, and personal banking under one team
cons: Likely requires substantial minimum assets under management—not accessible to average or middle-income consumers
Limited transparency on the website about fee structures, account minimums, or specific investment philosophy
As part of Merrill Lynch institutional framework, may lack the boutique or independent advisor flexibility some clients prefer
Website does not clearly articulate differentiation from other Merrill Lynch wealth management teams
No detailed case studies or specific client success metrics published to demonstrate track record
best_for: Business owners and entrepreneurs managing equity compensation and planning business transitions
High-net-worth individuals and families seeking comprehensive wealth planning and investment management
Corporate executives and financial professionals in the New York City area requiring specialized financial advice
Clients seeking integrated banking, lending, and investment services through a single established team
diagnosis: The Lipski Group is a high-end wealth management practice exclusively serving affluent clients, business owners, and executives—not a consumer credit or debt management service. This listing is fundamentally miscategorized as "credit-repair" and should not appear on CreditDoc, which serves consumers with credit challenges, debt issues, and affordable financial products. This is a premium private advisory firm requiring substantial assets and serving an entirely different consumer segment.
services: Lending products and personal credit facilities
Life insurance and financial protection products
Educational resources and market insights through The BullPen
